reasoning: Han Duck-soo is currently the Acting President of South Korea following Yoon Suk-yeol's impeachment, and he is 75 years old. Although he recently stated on April 22 that he has made "no decision yet" amid mounting calls from civic groups and some PPP factions to run, significant structural and political barriers make a candidacy unlikely. Most notably, he already skipped the standard PPP primary registration deadline on April 15, and he previously signaled on April 14 that he intended to focus on his current duties to address the US tariff crisis as his "final calling." Running would also require him to resign as Acting President, causing severe political instability right before the June 3 snap election, which would likely face severe public backlash. While a late draft or independent run is possible given the desperation of conservative factions trailing frontrunner Lee Jae-myung, the overall likelihood remains low.
